title: Novel combinatory method for surface and crystallinity analysis of crystalline materials
author: Matkivskyi, Røyset, et al.
description: This work is dedicated to developing a method of combined surface morphology- and crystallographic analysis for crystalline silicon. To demonstrate the applicability of the method, a series of chemical operations, such as polishing and texturing, were applied to multi-crystalline silicon samples. The samples were pre- and post-analysed with WLI and Laue techniques, and the experimental data allowed construction of maps for crystal orientation to etching rate dependency. The study illustrates the strengths of the combinatory technique as an alternative to existing techniques such as atom force microscopy (AFM) and electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD).•Combination of LAUE tool and white light interferometry techniques.•Alternative time-effective method to EBSD.•Analysis of surface morphology and crystallographic properties for chemical processing.

steps_raw.title: Method validation
steps_raw.instruction: The two tools described above were combined to determine the etching response of individual crystals in multi-crystalline Si wafers to different etching solutions. The method consisted of the main experimental steps: wafer polishing, Laue tool scanning, WLI scanning, wet chemical etching, second round WLI scanning as illustrated in Fig. 3.

steps_raw.title: Sample preparation (polishing of the Si surface)
steps_raw.instruction: To prepare the as-cut mc-Si wafer samples, chemical cleaning and subsequent polishing are required. The multi-crystalline Si wafers were laser cut into 4 × 4 cm2 pieces and cleaned in the RCA1 solution at 65-70°C and dried.
